data.title: United Launch Alliance CEO resigns amid rising rocket competition
data.description:
Tory Bruno stepped down as chief executive of United Launch Alliance, ending a tenure of nearly 12 years at the helm of the Boeing (BA)-Lockheed Martin (LMT) rocket joint venture.

His exit comes at a pivotal moment for ULA, one of the primary launch providers for U.S. national security missions and commercial customers such as Amazon. The company has been working to increase launch cadence while facing intensifying competition from SpaceX (SPACE) and other emerging rivals.

ULA said its chief operating officer, John Elbon, has been appointed interim chief executive, effective immediately.

The joint venture is among a small group of launch providers authorized to deploy the most sensitive U.S. military satellites. However, SpaceX has steadily expanded its share of the launch market, challenging ULA’s long-held position. The Elon Musk-led company is also weighing an initial public offering that could take place as early as 2026.

During Bruno’s leadership, ULA retired its legacy Delta and Atlas rocket families while advancing development of a next-generation launch vehicle known as Vulcan. The program, however, encountered repeated delays. Initially unveiled in 2015 with an expected first flight around 2019, Vulcan did not make its debut until January 2024.

Since then, the rocket has flown only two additional missions. Following an anomaly during its second launch in October 2024, ULA paused flights until August 2025, when Vulcan returned to service.

In early 2025, a U.S. Air Force assessment found that ULA had fallen short of performance expectations under its National Security Space Launch commitments. The company later received formal certification from the U.S. Space Force in March 2025, allowing Vulcan to carry military payloads.

Alongside Vulcan operations, ULA has continued flying its Atlas V rocket, completing five missions in 2025. These included a December launch carrying satellites for Amazon’s low-Earth-orbit broadband constellation.

Bruno’s departure also comes as competitive pressure in the launch sector continues to build. Blue Origin, backed by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos, is preparing to ramp up flights of its New Glenn rocket, while SpaceX continues development of its next-generation Starship vehicle.

In a statement, ULA’s board thanked Bruno for his leadership and service, saying he is leaving the company to pursue another opportunity.

data.title: Invesco is the top performing asset management stock YTD
data.description:
The S&P 500 financials sector has largely kept pace with the broader market in 2025, rising about +14% year to date.

Financials rank as the fourth-best performing sector in the S&P 500 (SP500).

As we look at the sub-sectors within the broader financial sector, below is a list of Asset Management and Custody Banks stocks ranked by their YTD performance. The list includes SA Quant ratings for each stock.

The list is topped by Invesco (IVZ), with a YTD performance of +56.41% and a Strong Buy Quant Rating of 4.83. Affiliated Managers Group (AMG) and The Bank of New York Mellon (BK) follow closely behind with YTD gains of +55.56% and +52.62%, respectively.

Northern Trust (NTRS) at +36.84% and Patria Investments (PAX) at +34.82% round out the top five performers. Notably, most of the top-performing stocks carry a Hold Quant Rating, with Invesco (IVZ) being the exception with its Strong Buy rating. Janus Henderson Group (JHG), ranking 12th with an +11.57% YTD gain, also carries a Strong Buy rating of 4.52.

Here is the list:

  1. Invesco (IVZ), YTD perf: +56.41%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.83)

  2. Affiliated Managers Group (AMG), YTD perf: +55.56%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.38)

data.title: Brown & Brown cut to Market Perform as BMO sees softer organic growth
data.description:
Brown & Brown (BRO) shares were downgraded to Market Perform on Tuesday as analyst Michael Zaremski sees softer-than-consensus organic growth.

He lowered his 2026 organic growth estimate by 60 basis points to +2.7%, compared with the Wall Street consensus of +3.8%. The analyst also reduced his 2027 estimate by 120 bps to +3.2%, vs. +3.8% consensus, while noting BRO's valuation has a strong relationship with organic growth rates.

"We furthermore estimate that consensus will only lower BRO's organic estimates by a materially more muted level, which could act as a sentiment overhand amongst certain investors in '26," Zaremski wrote in a note to clients.

BRO shares slipped 1.4% in midday trading.

BMO's Market Perform rate compares with the average SA analyst rating of Buy, as well as the average Wall Street analyst rating and the SA Quant system rating, both at Hold.

data.title: Boat stocks set sail as Loop Capital initates coverage over industry optimism
data.description:
Loop Capital initiated coverage on marine industry stocks, kicking off MarineMax (HZO) and Malibu Boats (MBUU) with bullish ratings and starting neutrally on MasterCraft Boat (MCFT) and Brunswick (BC). The research firm believes shares of these companies reflect attractive entry points from a valuation perspective.

Loop is optimistic on the marine industry going into 2026, despite the unfavorable macro backdrop and elevated interest rate environment, as the sector has undergone a significant inventory rationalization period over the past 12-18 months, which appears to be in the late innings entering 2026.

Boat and yacht retailer MarineMax is "the name to own in the boating industry," Loop said, which has prime retail locations, exclusive brands, underappreciated high-margin businesses, and exposure to the premium boating consumer. The company is poised to exceed investor expectations in 2026, driven by a strong management team and reasonable/conservative guidance, the research firm said.

HZO has a "buy" rating and a price target of $29, implying an upside of 17.5%. 

On Malibu Boats, Loop expects the company's strong portfolio of brands, vertical integration capabilities, experienced management team, and strong balance sheet to fuel growth initiatives.

Malibu's vertical integration capabilities around boat production allow the company to drive efficiencies by managing assembly and design, Loop said.  MBUU is rated "buy" with a PT of $34, implying an upside of 17.5%.

On MasterCraft Boat, the research firm believes the shares are valued fairly at current levels and views the company's management team and the efforts to rationalize dealer inventories favorably.

"We would not hesitate to get more constructive on MCFT given evidence of improving underlying retail fundamentals and/or a meaningful pullback in the shares that provides a more attractive entry point," the research firm said in its note dated December 22.

MCFT has a "hold" rating and a PT of $21, implying an upside of 7.5%.

Loop has noted that Brunswick has a strong management team with a portfolio of elite marine engine and boat brands, but its shares are up more than 36% over the last six months amid an uncertain macro backdrop. The research firm believes a meaningful pullback in the shares should provide a more attractive entry point.

BC is rated "hold" with a PT of $74, implying a downside of 3.4%.

data.title: Silver tops all-time high of $70/oz and these mining companies have taken notice
data.description:
Silver mining stocks surged as the precious metal (XAGUSD:CUR) topped $70 per ounce for the first time, sparking renewed investor interest in the sector.

In response, Seeking Alpha highlighted mining companies demonstrating strong quantitative metrics. Leading the pack is Santacruz Silver Mining (SCZMD), boasting a Quant Rating of 4.98, signaling robust performance potential. Close behind was Guardian Metal Resources (GMTLF) and Nexa Resources (NEXA), both carrying Strong Buy ratings, reflecting solid fundamentals and growth prospects. 

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Ratings system grades stocks based on their relative performance on critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any rating of 3.5 or above considered a bullish rating and any rating of 2.5 or below considered a bearish rating. 

Here is the list: 

No. 1: Santacruz Silver Mining (SCZMD): Quant Rating of 4.98 (Strong Buy), +1,053.9% YTD.

No. 2: Guardian Metal Resources (GMTLF): Quant Rating: 4.97 (Strong Buy), +423% YTD.

No. 3: Nexa Resources (NEXA): Quant Rating: 4.85 (Strong Buy), +1.5% YTD. 

No. 4: Amerigo Resources (ARREF): Quant Rating: 4.80 (Strong Buy), +187.6% YTD. 

No. 5: Hudbay Minerals (HBM): Quant Rating: 4.78 (Strong Buy), +145% YTD. 

No. 6: Ivanhoe Electric (IE): Quant Rating: 4.49 (Buy), +119.2% YTD. 

No. 7: Glencore (GLNCY): Quant Rating: 4.00 (Buy), +20.7% YTD. 

No. 8: Compass Minerals International (CMP): Quant Rating: 3.89 (Buy), +80.1% YTD. 

No. 9: Teck Resources Limited (TECK): Quant Rating: 3.87 (Buy), +15% YTD. 

No. 10: Contango Ore (CTGO): Quant Rating: 3.49 (Hold), +191.2% YTD. 

Silver and Silver Mining ETFs: (SLV), (SIVR), (AGQ), (ZSL), (PSLV), (SIL), (SILJ), and (SLVP).

data.title: Coty's CEO succession creates worrisome disruptions -- Evercore ISI
data.description:
Coty (COTY) is preparing for a new CEO at a critical juncture, a shift Evercore ISI said raises uncertainty and led the firm to downgrade the cosmetics company to In-Line from Outperform.

“CEO Sue Nabi has been central to Coty’s investment thesis,” Evercore ISI analyst Robert Ottenstein says, citing a lack of visibility on the timing and catalyst for a “potential value unlock” in the shares.

“We expect Coty to remain undervalued and see outperformance unlikely,” Ottenstein adds.

Nabi publicly announced her decision to step down from Coty (COTY) on December 22 and will be succeeded on an interim basis by Markus Strobel on January 1. Strobel will also assume the role of executive chairman from Peter Harf, after Nabi and Harf were allegedly pushed out by majority shareholder JAB Holdings.  

Strobel comes to Coty (COTY) from Procter & Gamble (PG) where he was president of P&G’s skin and personal care business. Strobel will be tasked with addressing the challenges at Coty’s (COTY) Consumer division, “a business that accounts for 15% of profits but nearly all of the operational challenges and bearish sentiment,” Ottenstein says.

“Coty’s first challenge was CoverGirl’s loss of shelf space without Procter’s commercial leverage with Target; the merger revealed diseconomies of scale, which arguably opened the door to e.l.f. Beauty,” he adds.

Two issues that will require Strobel’s full attention: competition from more popular “indie” beauty brands and the much larger L’Oreal; CoverGirl’s reliance on “disrupted” drugstores; and the fragrance business, which, under Nabi, remained competitive.

“Fragrances require creativity and speed, which leadership changes like this often interrupt,” Ottenstein says, reminding investors that prestige fragrances (i.e. Burberry, Marc Jacobs, Chloe) account for nearly 85% of Coty’s (COTY) profits.

Analysts are split on Coty (COTY) with Seeking Alpha authors viewing the stock as a Buy, but Wall Street analysts giving the stock a Hold rating. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, however views Coty (COTY) as a Sell with a score of 1.83 out of 5.

Coty (COTY) shares were down as much as 6% on Tuesday, setting a five year low.

data.title: Altcoins on pace for poor 2025 returns as crypto market cap dips
data.description:
Many of the major altcoins are tracking double-digit year-to-date losses as the broader crypto market cap slumped, signaling sustained weakness beyond bitcoin (BTC-USD).

The total crypto market value, mostly comprising bitcoin (BTC-USD) and ether (ETH-USD), stood at $2.97T on Tuesday, down roughly 10% from $3.32T at the start of the year, according to CoinMarketCap data. BTC was down ~7% YTD, while ETH was off ~12%, as investor demand weakened after strong gains earlier in the year, when BTC hit new record highs. 

The biggest altcoins by market cap (excluding ETH) and their YTD returns as of Tuesday midday trading: BNB (BNB-USD) +20%, XRP (XRP-USD) -19%, Solana (SOL-USD) -37%, Dogecoin (DOGE-USD) -60%, Cardano (ADA-USD) -61%, Chainlink (LINK-USD) -44%, Hyperliquid (HYPE-USD) -9%, Monero (XMR-USD) +126%, Stellar (XLM-USD) -49%, Litecoin (LTC-USD) -28%, Avalanche (AVAX-USD) -68% and Shiba Inu (SHIB-USD) -67%.

With the year coming to an end, investors are assessing whether the broad crypto weakness sustains or flips in 2026. Citi Research has contended that bitcoin's (BTC-USD) performance will reverse next year on the view that greater regulatory clarity will continue to boost digital asset adoption.

Gracy Chen, CEO of crypto trading platform Bitget, said that bitcoin (BTC-USD) "turned into the market's anchor asset" this year, pushing other cryptos to stand on their own merits and revealing clear gaps in strength across the altcoin space.

"Some assets didn’t need reinvention," she added. "Yet, many other projects, particularly in AI, DeFi, and Layer-1, that performed well in 2024, discovered that the old hype couldn’t let them succeed in the long run. That’s where selectivity really shows. As 2026 comes closer, we can’t say there’ll be a classic altseason like in 2020/2021," during which a speculative frenzy pushed capital from bitcoin into altcoins.

SteelWave Digital Founder Mitchell DiRaimondo is calling 2025 "a necessary reset" for altcoins. "What we are watching now is the system clearing out projects that never should have survived a higher rate, higher scrutiny environment." Projects that make it through next year will hinge on fundamentals, and "the capital will come to them naturally once institutions see structure, yield, and risk controls that look familiar."

data.title: Ørsted sells majority stake in Taiwanese wind farm
data.description:
Ørsted (DNNGY) (DOGEF) agreed to sell a controlling stake in one of its offshore wind projects in Taiwan to Cathay, as the Danish energy group continues efforts to strengthen its balance sheet.

Cathay Life Insurance, together with its affiliate Cathay Power, will acquire a 55% stake in the Greater Changhua 2 offshore wind project for roughly 5 billion Danish kroner ($789 million), Ørsted said in a statement on Tuesday. The transaction lifts the company’s asset sales this year to about 33 billion kroner.

The sale comes as Ørsted seeks to bolster its financial position following a series of challenges, including difficulties in the U.S. offshore wind market that have weighed on earnings and prompted the company to pursue additional funding, including a large capital raise. The broader offshore wind sector has also been under pressure from higher costs and supply-chain disruptions, factors that have contributed to asset write-downs and management changes at the company.

The Greater Changhua 2 wind farm is located roughly 35 miles off Taiwan’s Changhua County. The project consists of two phases: Greater Changhua 2a, which is already in operation, and Greater Changhua 2b, which remains under construction.

data.title: Pentagon will add xAI's Grok to its AI service in early 2026
data.description:
The U.S. Dept. of Defense said late on Monday that it will add xAI's (X.AI) Grok family of artificial intelligence models to its GenAI.mil service next year.

“Targeted for initial deployment in early 2026, this integration will allow all military and civilian personnel to use xAI's capabilities at Impact Level 5 (IL5), enabling the secure handling of Controlled Unclassified Information in daily workflows,” the Dept. of Defense said in a statement. “Users will also gain access to real‑time global insights from the X platform, providing War Department personnel with a decisive information advantage.”

“The War Department will continue scaling an AI ecosystem built for speed, security, and decision superiority,” the department continued. “Newly IL5-certified capabilities will empower every aspect of the Department's workforce, turning AI into a daily operational asset.”

The Grok family of models will be available to the department's 3M civilian and military personnel and will be used for productivity boosts, including summarizing policy handbooks, generating checklists, getting key terms from statements of work and more.

Earlier this month, Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) said that its Gemini for government service will become the first enterprise AI deployed for the Dept. of Defense. The department has been rebranded to the Dept. of War by President Trump.
